Latest 10-K business section (newest first):
  filing_date=2022-08-05 section=business period_end=2022-06-30
Item 1.  Business.
The Procter & Gamble Company (the Company) is focused on providing branded products of superior quality and value to improve the lives of the world's consumers, now and for generations to come.  The Company was incorporated in Ohio in 1905, having first been established as a New Jersey corporation in 1890, and was built from a business founded in Cincinnati in 1837 by William Procter and James Gamble.  Today, our products are sold in approximately 180 countries and territories.
Additional information required by this item is incorporated herein by reference to Management's Discussion and Analysis (MD&A); and Notes 1 and 2 to our Consolidated Financial Statements.  Unless the context indicates otherwise, the terms the "Company," "P&G," "we," "our" or "us" as used herein refer to The Procter & Gamble Company (the registrant) and its subsidiaries.
Throughout this Form 10-K, we incorporate by reference information from other documents filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC).
The Company's Annual Report on Form 10-K, quarterly reports on Form 10-Q and current reports on Form 8-K, and amendments thereto, are filed electronically with the SEC.  The SEC maintains an internet site that contains these reports at: www.sec.gov.  Reports can also be accessed through links from our website at: www.pginvestor.com.  P&G includes the website link solely as a textual reference.  The information contained on our website is not incorporated by reference into this report.
Copies of these reports are also available, without charge, by contacting EQ Shareowner Services, 1100 Centre Pointe Curve, Suite 101, Mendota, MN 55120-4100.
Financial Information about Segments
Information about our reportable segments can be found in the MD&A and Note 2 to our Consolidated Financial Statements.
Narrative Description of Business
Business Model
.  Our business model relies on the continued growth and success of existing brands and products, as well as the creation of new innovative products and brands.  The markets and industry segments in which we offer our products are highly competitive.  Our products are sold in approximately 180 countries and territories through numerous channels as well as direct-to-consumer.  Our growth strategy is to deliver meaningful and noticeable superiority across five key vectors of our consumer proposition - product performance, packaging, brand communication, retail execution and consumer and customer value.  We use our research and development (R&D) and consumer insights to provide superior products and packaging.  We utilize our marketing and online presence to deliver superior brand messaging to our co
…[truncated 2138 chars]…
 these patents or licenses cover significant product formulation and processes used to manufacture our products.  The trademarks are important to the overall marketing and branding of our products.  All major trademarks in each business are registered.  In part, our success can be attributed to the existence and continued protection of these trademarks, patents and licenses.
Competitive Condition
.  The markets in which our products are sold are highly competitive.  Our products compete against similar products of many large and small companies, including well-known global competitors.  In many of the markets and industry segments in which we sell our products, we compete against other branded products as well as retailers' private-label brands.  We are well positioned in the industry segments and markets in which we operate, often holding a leadership or significant market share position.  We support our products with advertising, promotions and other marketing vehicles to build awareness
2        The Procter & Gamble Company
and trial of our brands and products in conjunction with our sales force.  We believe this combination provides the most efficient method of marketing for these types of products.  Product quality, performance, value and packaging are also important differentiating factors.
Government Regulation
.  Our Company is subject to a wide variety of laws and regulations across the countries in which we do business.  In the United States, many of our products and manufacturing operations are subject to one or more federal or state regulatory agencies, including the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA), the Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA), the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) and the Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC).  We are also subject to anti-corruption laws and regulations, such as the U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act, and antitrust and competition laws and regulations that govern our dealings with suppliers, customers, competitors and government officials.
In addition, many foreign jurisdictions in which we do business have regulations and regulatory bodies that govern similar aspects of our operations and products, in some cases to an even more significant degree.  We are also subject to expanding laws and regulations related to environmental protection and other sustainability-related matters, non-financial reporting and diligence, labor and employment, trade, taxation and data privacy and protection, including the European Union's General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and similar regulations in states within the United States and in countries around the world.  For additional information on the potential impacts of global legal and regulatory requirements on our business, see "Item 1A. Risk Factors" herein.
The Company has in place compliance programs and internal and external experts to help guide our business in complying with these and other existing laws and regulations that apply to us around the globe; and we have made, and plan to continue making, necessary expenditures for compliance with these laws and regulations.  We also expect that our many suppliers, consultants and other third parties working on our behalf share our commitment to compliance, and we have policies and procedures in place to manage these relationships, though they inherently involve a lesser degree of control over operations and governance.  We do not expect that the Company's expenditures for compliance with current government regulations, including current environmental regulations, will have a material effect on our total capital expenditures, earnings or competitive position in fiscal year 2023 as compared to prior periods.
Human Capital
.  Our employees are a key source of competitive advantage.  Their actions, guided by our Purpose, Values and Principles (PVPs), are critical to the long-term success of our business.  We aim to retain our talented employees by offering competitive compensation and benefits, strong career development and a respectful and inclusive culture that provides equal opportunity for all.
Our Board of Directors, through the Compensation and Leadership Development Committee (C&LD Committee), provides oversight of the Company's policies and strategies relating to talent including diversity, equality and inclusion as well as the Company's compensation principles and practices.  The C&LD Committee also evaluates and approves the Company's compensation plans, policies and programs applicable to our senior executives.
Employees
As of June 30, 2022, the Company had approximately 106,000 employees, an increase of five percent versus the prior year due primarily to business growth.  The total number of employees is an estimate of total Company employees excluding interns, co-ops, contractors and employees of joint ventures.  49% of our employees are in manufacturing roles and 26% of our employees are located in the United States.  41% of our global employees are women.  As of June 30, 2022, 28% of our U.S. employees identify as multicultural.
Training and Development
We focus on attracting, developing and retaining skilled and diverse talent, both from universities and the broader market.  We recruit from among the best universities across markets in which we compete and are generally able to select from the top talent.  We focus on developing our employees by providing a variety of job experiences, training programs and skill development opportunities.  Given our develop-from-within model for staffing most of our senior leadership positions, it is particularly important for us to ensure holistic growth and full engagement of our employees.
Diversity, Equality and Inclusion
As a consumer products company, we believe that it is important for our workforce to reflect the diversity of our consumers worldwide.  We also seek to foster an inclusive work environment where each individual can bring their authentic self, which helps drive innovation and enables us to better serve our consumers.  We aspire to achieve equal gender representation globally and at key management and leadership levels.  Within the U.S. workforce, our aspiration is to achieve 40% multicultural representation overall as well as at management and leadership levels.
Compensation and Benefits
Our compensation plans are based on the principles of paying for performance, paying competitively versus peer companies that we compete with for talent and in the marketplace and focusing on long-term success through a combination of short-term and long-term incentive programs.  We also offer competitive benefit programs, including retirement plans and health insurance in line with local country practices with flexibility to accommodate the needs of a diverse workforce.
Sustainability
.  Environmental sustainability is a key focus area and integrated into P&G's business strategies.  The Company has declared its focus on developing irresistibly superior products and packages that are sustainable.  The Company announced an ambition to reduce greenhouse gas emissions, purchase renewable electricity for our operations, reduce our use of virgin petroleum-based plastic in our packaging, increase the recyclability or reusability of our packaging and increase responsible sourcing of key forest-based commodities such as wood pulp and palm oil.
The Procter & Gamble Company        3
Additional detailed information on our sustainability efforts including our TCFD (Task Force on Climate-Related Financial Disclosures), SASB (Sustainability Accounting Standards Board) and CDP (Carbon Disclosure Project) reports can be found on our website at https://pginvestor.com/esg.  References to our sustainability reports and website are for informational purposes only and neither the sustainability reports nor the other information on our website is incorporated by reference into this Annual Report on Form 10-K.
PG.quantitative_report:
# PG — Quantitative Report (Decision Date: 2023-04-01)

## 1. Financial Trajectory

**Revenue** is essentially flat. Q2 FY2023 (Dec-2022) revenue of $20.77B compares to $20.95B one year prior (YoY −0.9%). TTM revenue stands at $80.28B. Sequentially, QoQ growth was a modest +0.8% ($20.61B → $20.77B).

**Earnings** are declining. Q2 EPS of $1.59 is down −4.2% YoY from $1.66 (Dec-2021). Net income fell −6.7% YoY ($4.24B → $3.96B). TTM net income is $14.35B.

**Margins are compressing:**
- Gross margin: 47.6% in Q2 (9.88B / 20.77B) versus 49.1% a year earlier (10.29B / 20.95B), a −150 bps contraction. Gross profit declined −4.0% YoY.
- Operating margin: 23.1% in Q2 (4.79B / 20.77B) versus 24.7% a year earlier (5.17B / 20.95B). TTM operating income: $17.35B.
- Net margin: 17.9% (per valuation panel), down from 18.5% in Apr-2022.

**Cash flow:** All quarterly cash-flow data are unavailable. TTM free-cash-flow is unavailable.

**Balance sheet** is stable. Total equity: $44.73B (Dec-2022), ranging $44.33B–$46.92B over eight quarters. Total liabilities: $72.99B (liabilities-to-equity ratio: 1.63x). Total assets: $117.72B, broadly steady in the $117B–$121B range.

## 2. Earnings Quality & Balance-Sheet Resilience

**Earnings quality signals are mixed.** Net margin is high at 17.9% but has drifted lower (from 18.5%). Gross-margin compression (−150 bps YoY) suggests cost pressure that has not yet been fully offset by pricing or mix. ROE remains strong and stable at 32.1% (range over the valuation window: 0.316–0.330). Without cash-flow statements, cash conversion and free-cash-flow coverage cannot be verified — this is a meaningful gap in assessing earnings quality.

**Balance-sheet resilience is adequate.** Equity base of $44.73B provides a solid cushion; total liabilities of $72.99B imply a manageable leverage profile relative to the asset base of $117.72B. Equity has not eroded materially over the eight-quarter window.

## 3. Valuation vs. Own History

| Metric | Latest (Mar-2023) | 12-Month Range | Position in Range |
|---|---|---|---|
| P/E diluted | 25.7x | 21.3x – 28.3x | 63rd percentile (mid-high) |
| P/B | 8.25x | 6.7x – 9.1x | 65th percentile |
| P/S | 4.60x | n/a specific range | — |
| Earnings yield | 3.9% | — | — |

The P/E of 25.7x sits in the upper half of its one-year range. It is not at a trough level (trough was 21.3x–21.9x in Oct-2022). Given that earnings are declining (YoY EPS −4.2%) and margins are compressing, the current multiple is not discounted relative to deteriorating fundamentals — it is priced for stability that the numbers do not yet confirm.

## 4. Price Level & Return Context

- **Latest close:** $148.69 (Mar-31, 2023)
- **52-week range:** $122.18 – $164.90
- **Quarterly return:** −2.6% (from $152.59 at Dec-29)
- **Annual return:** −3.7% (from $154.39 at Mar-30, 2022)

The price path has been volatile: a −10.5% drop Q2 2022 (Jun → Sep, from $143.79 to $128.70), a sharp +18.6% snap-back Q3 2022 (Sep → Dec), then a mild −2.6% decline in the latest quarter. The stock is currently $148.69, 9.9% below its 52-week high of $164.90 and 21.7% above its low of $122.18.

Price has modestly underperformed fundamentals: the stock is down −3.7% annually while TTM revenue and earnings remain large but are decelerating on a YoY basis.

## 5. Strongest Quantitative Upside and Downside

**Upside drivers (quantitative):**
- High, stable profitability: ROE of 32.1%, net margin of 17.9%, operating margin of 23.1% — these are defensive quality metrics.
- If P/E reverts toward the upper bound of 28.3x on stable TTM EPS of ~$5.71 (sum of last four quarters), implied price ≈ $161.6, representing +8.7% upside from $148.69.
- Revenue has stabilized sequentially (+0.8% QoQ), and TTM revenue of $80.28B is a solid base.

**Downside risks (quantitative):**
- YoY earnings decline of −4.2% (EPS) and −6.7% (net income) with margin compression accelerating.
- If P/E contracts to the trough of 21.3x on further EPS erosion (e.g., TTM EPS falling to ~$5.50), implied price ≈ $117.2, representing −21.2% downside.
- Gross margin at 46.6% (valuation panel) is at the lower end of its recent history; continued input-cost pressure could further erode earnings.
- No free-cash-flow data available — inability to confirm that reported earnings translate to cash.

## 6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion

PG presents a stable-earnings, high-quality compounder at a mid-to-upper valuation range. The P/E of 25.7x is not cheap for a business showing YoY revenue decline (−0.9%), EPS decline (−4.2%), and margin compression (gross margin −150 bps YoY). The balance sheet is resilient, and profitability metrics (ROE 32.1%, net margin 17.9%) remain strong, but these are priced in. Upside to the upper valuation bound (~+8.7%) is modest; downside to a trough multiple with further earnings erosion is materially larger (~−21%). The absence of cash-flow data limits conviction on earnings quality. On a purely quantitative basis, the risk/reward is asymmetric to the downside at this valuation level given the fundamental deceleration trend.
PG.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Assessment Report: Procter & Gamble (PG)**
**Assessment Date:** 2023-04-01

**1. Business/Franchise Read (from 2022-08-05 10-K):**
Procter & Gamble is a global consumer staples company selling branded products in approximately 180 countries. Its business model is built on sustaining and growing its portfolio of leading brands through a strategy of "meaningful and noticeable superiority" across product performance, packaging, brand communication, retail execution, and value. The company operates in highly competitive markets against both other global brands and private-label products. It maintains a broad distribution network with significant customer concentration, where Walmart accounted for approximately 15% of total sales (2020-2022). The company emphasizes R&D, consumer insights, and productivity improvements to drive balanced growth. Human capital and sustainability are highlighted as key strategic focus areas.

**2. Recent Developments (Dated, from supplied text):**
*   **Guidance/Outlook (Referenced January 2023):** Management projected organic sales growth of between 4% and 5% for fiscal year 2023, a modest increase from prior guidance (referenced in articles from 2023-03-16 and 2023-03-19).
*   **Market Performance & Valuation Commentary (2023-03-23):** News noted shares were down 12% from their high but characterized the stock as "not exactly cheap" and "rarely goes on sale," suggesting a perception of limited discount despite the pullback.
*   **Competitive Positioning & Recession Resilience (2023-03-29):** A comparative article positioned P&G as likely to perform well through a recession, underscoring its defensive qualities.
*   **ESG & Activist Pressure (2023-03-16):** Investor groups urged shareholders to vote for a proposal requiring the company to report on its efforts to address deforestation in its supply chain, highlighting reputational and governance risk.
*   **Product Safety & Legal Risk (2023-02-10):** Reports of benzene contamination in dry shampoo products raised the potential for liability lawsuits.

**3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets:**
*   **Risk: Regulatory & Legal Exposure.** The company is subject to extensive global regulation (FDA, EPA, FTC, GDPR) and faces specific product liability litigation related to benzene (2023-02-10) and environmental scrutiny over deforestation (2023-03-16). These can lead to financial penalties, reputational damage, and operational constraints.
    *   *Offset:* The 10-K states compliance expenditures are not expected to be material to capital or earnings, and the company has established compliance programs.
*   **Risk: Intense Competition & Market Saturation.** The 10-K confirms markets are "highly competitive" against global brands and private labels. News articles (2023-03-23) note challenges in a "highly saturated market," which can pressure market share and pricing power.
    *   *Offset:* The company asserts it holds leadership or significant share positions and competes through sustained investment in superiority vectors.
*   **Risk: Supply Chain & Input Cost Volatility.** Reliance on third-party raw materials, including single-source suppliers, and exposure to fluctuating commodity prices for chemicals, fuel, and natural gas pose margin risk (2022-08-05 10-K).
    *   *Offset:* The company's scale and procurement capabilities are inherent strengths, though the text does not specify mitigation beyond noting prices are subject to fluctuation.
*   **Risk: Customer Concentration.** Significant dependence on Walmart (15% of sales) and the top 10 customers (39% of sales) creates negotiation pressure and vulnerability to retailer strategy shifts (2022-08-05 10-K).
    *   *Offset:* The company sells through diverse channels, including direct-to-consumer, and maintains collaborative relationships with key customers.

**4. Concise Qualitative Assessment:**
**Neutral.** P&G's franchise is demonstrably strong, characterized by global scale, iconic brands, and a defensive, recession-resilient profile. Recent guidance for mid-single-digit organic growth is stable, if not particularly robust. However, the provided evidence does not reveal a compelling near-term positive catalyst beyond stability. The valuation is noted as not cheap, and the identified risks—legal/regulatory actions, sustained competitive intensity, and macro input cost volatility—are non-trivial and could pressure margins or market perception. The balance of a superior business model against a muted near-term outlook and tangible risks supports a neutral qualitative stance.

corrections.issue: Quant report states 'Gross margin: 47.6% in Q2 (9.88B / 20.77B)' but the valuation panel in the raw evidence shows gross_margin=0.466 (46.6%). The Q2 calculation (9.88/20.77=47.6%) is arithmetically correct for that quarter, but the report also references 'Gross margin at 46.6% (valuation panel)' in the downside section without clarifying these are different measures (quarterly vs. trailing/valuation-panel). This creates a minor inconsistency that could confuse the reader about which gross margin figure is authoritative.
corrections.required_action: Clarify that 47.6% is the single-quarter Q2 gross margin while 46.6% is the valuation-panel figure (likely trailing or blended), and note the discrepancy explicitly.

corrections.issue: Quant report states 'Net margin: 17.9% (per valuation panel), down from 18.5% in Apr-2022.' The raw evidence sparse history shows net_margin=0.185 on 2022-04-01 and net_margin=0.179 on 2023-03-31, which is consistent. However, the report also states 'operating margin: 23.1% in Q2 (4.79B / 20.77B)' while the valuation panel shows operating_margin=0.216 (21.6%). The Q2 single-quarter calculation (4.79/20.77=23.1%) is correct, but the valuation-panel operating margin of 21.6% is notably lower and not discussed. This gap suggests the trailing operating margin is weaker than the latest quarter, which would strengthen the bearish case but is omitted.
corrections.required_action: Acknowledge the valuation-panel operating margin of 21.6% and explain the difference from the 23.1% quarterly figure, as the trailing metric is more relevant for valuation context.

corrections.issue: Qual report states 'News noted shares were down 12% from their high' referencing 2023-03-23 article. The quant evidence shows the 52-week high of $164.90 and the 2023-03-23 price is not directly given, but the 2023-03-28 close was $146.36. From $164.90 to $146.36 is an ~11.2% decline, and the 2023-03-31 close of $148.69 is ~9.9% below the high. The '12% from high' claim is from the news article itself and is approximately consistent with the price data, so this is acceptable as a cited source claim. No correction needed on this point.
corrections.required_action: No action needed — the 12% figure is sourced from the news article and is approximately consistent with available price data.

unsupported_claims: The quant report's downside scenario assumes 'TTM EPS falling to ~$5.50' without evidence from the supplied packs that EPS is on a trajectory to decline to that level. The current TTM EPS (sum of last 4 quarters: 1.59+1.57+1.22+1.33=5.71) is $5.71, and the YoY decline rate of -4.2% does not mechanically imply a drop to $5.50. This is a scenario assumption, not a claim supported by the raw evidence, and should be labeled as such.

The qual report's statement that 'the provided evidence does not reveal a compelling near-term positive catalyst beyond stability' is a reasonable inference but slightly understates the evidence: the raw qualitative packs include a 2023-03-03 article noting an analyst turning bullish and expecting headwinds in Care and Feminine Care to 'abate this calendar year, allowing PG to build on its share growth momentum.' This is a potential catalyst present in the supplied evidence that is not acknowledged in the qual report's assessment.

The risk/reward is roughly balanced: modest upside to upper valuation bounds (~+8.7%) is offset by larger downside if earnings erosion continues and multiples contract toward trough levels (~-21%).
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: PG is a high-quality defensive franchise with strong profitability (ROE 32.1%, net margin 17.9%) but is priced at a mid-to-upper valuation (P/E 25.7x, 63rd percentile) while fundamentals are decelerating—YoY EPS decline of -4.2%, revenue flat at -0.9% YoY, and gross margin compression of -150 bps. The risk/reward is roughly balanced: modest upside to upper valuation bounds (~+8.7%) is offset by larger downside if earnings erosion continues and multiples contract toward trough levels (~-21%).

MMM.quantitative_report:
**Quantitative Report: MMM (3M) as of 2023-04-01**

**1. Financial Trajectory**
*   **Revenue:** Declining quarterly trajectory. Q4 2022 revenue was $8.08B, down -6.3% QoQ from $8.62B and -6.2% YoY from $8.61B. TTM revenue is $34.23B.
*   **Earnings & Margins:** Significant earnings volatility and margin compression. Q4 2022 net income was $543.00M (EPS $1.03), down -85.9% QoQ and -59.5% YoY. TTM net income is $5.79B. Gross margin contracted to 43.2% in Q4 ($3.49B/$8.08B) vs. 45.4% in Q1 2022. Operating margin collapsed to 7.8% in Q4 ($632.00M/$8.08B) vs. 18.5% in Q1 2022. TTM net margin is 16.9%.
*   **Cash Flow:** Data unavailable for all periods.
*   **Balance Sheet:** Leverage is moderate but improving. Debt-to-Equity is 1.079 ($15.94B total debt / $14.77B total equity). Total debt has decreased from $17.35B at 2021-12-31 to $15.94B at 2022-12-31. Total equity has fluctuated, ending 2022 at $14.77B.

**2. Earnings Quality & Balance-Sheet Resilience**
*   **Earnings Quality:** Low quality. The extreme quarterly EPS volatility (range of $0.14 to $6.77 over the last eight quarters) suggests potential non-recurring items significantly impact results. The sharp disconnect between TTM net income ($5.79B) and recent quarterly results ($543.00M) indicates poor durability of reported earnings.
*   **Balance-Sheet Resilience:** Moderate. The debt reduction trend is positive. The Debt-to-Equity ratio of 1.079 is manageable but provides limited buffer given the deteriorating earnings power.

**3. Valuation vs. History**
*   Current valuation is at the low end of its recent range. As of 2023-03-31, the P/E (diluted) is 8.633, vs. a 12-month range of 8.1 to 17.5. The Price-to-Book ratio is 3.377, vs. a 12-month range of 3.2 to 5.3.
*   The low multiple is conditioned on the reported earnings. On a trailing basis (TTM net income $5.79B, implied market cap ~$50B based on P/E 8.6), the stock appears statistically cheap. However, the recent quarterly earnings run-rate ($543M annualized ≈ $2.2B) would imply a much higher P/E, questioning the valuation's stability.

**4. Price Level & Returns vs. Fundamentals**
*   The stock has experienced a severe decline, reflecting the fundamental deterioration. Price is $87.88 on 2023-03-31.
*   **Annual Return:** -30.6% (from $126.61 on 2022-03-30).
*   **Quarterly Return:** -12.8% (from $100.81 on 2022-12-29).
*   The price decline correlates with the collapse in quarterly earnings and revenue growth. The stock is trading near its 52-week low of $83.74.

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside & Downside**
*   **Strongest Upside:** Mean reversion in valuation multiples. The current P/E (8.6) and P/B (3.4) are at the floor of their recent history. A reversion to the median multiples observed in the provided history (P/E ~12, P/B ~4.2) would represent significant price appreciation, conditional on earnings stabilization.
*   **Strongest Downside:** Continuation of earnings deterioration. If the recent quarterly operating income (~$632M) and net income (~$543M) represent a new normalized level, current earnings yield (11.6%) is misleading. Further revenue declines or margin pressure could lead to additional earnings downgrades and balance-sheet stress, pushing valuations lower.

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**
The quantitative profile presents a conflict: the stock is trading at historically low valuation multiples, but this follows a sharp and ongoing deterioration in revenue, earnings, and margins. The balance sheet is stable but not exceptionally strong. The key risk is that low multiples reflect accurately anticipated continued fundamental weakness rather than an overreaction. The key reward is that current valuations have already priced in a significant amount of bad news, creating upside potential if the fundamental trajectory stabilizes. The provisional conclusion is **cautious**, as the cheap valuation appears to be a function of deteriorating fundamentals rather than a market oversight. A sustained recovery in quarterly earnings and revenue trends would be needed to justify the low multiple as a buying opportunity.
MMM.qualitative_report:
### Qualitative Assessment: 3M Company (MMM) – As of 2023-04-01

**1. Business/Franchise Read from Supplied Narrative**
From the 2023-02-08 10-K filing, 3M is characterized as a "diversified technology company" with a global presence operating across four business segments: Safety and Industrial; Transportation and Electronics; Health Care; and Consumer. The company maintains a portfolio of recognized brands (e.g., Scotch-Brite, Post-it, 3M™ VHB™ Tapes) and products distributed through a broad network of wholesalers, retailers, and directly to users. The 10-K identifies competitive advantages stemming from trademarks, patents, and trade secrets. A key strategic action announced in July 2022 is the intention to spin off the Health Care business as a separate public company.

**2. Recent Developments (Dated)**
*   **Corporate Strategy:** The planned spin-off of the Health Care segment, announced in July 2022, remains a forward-looking strategic action (10-K, 2023-02-08).
*   **Market & Sentiment (Mar 2023):** Financial commentary in late March 2023 presents a mixed view. Some articles cite potential for growth from "improving supply chain and cost management actions" and shareholder rewards (2023-03-30). Others note the stock's underperformance (2023-03-29, 2023-03-30), its status as technically "oversold" (2023-03-28), and describe investor caution due to "cloudy" outlook (2023-03-31). Analyst ratings as of 2023-03-22 were reported as neutral to somewhat bearish.
*   **Operational Context (Feb 2023):** The 10-K notes that in 2022, 3M experienced "raw material price inflation and constrained supply" due to geopolitical and logistics disruptions (2023-02-08).

**3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets**
*   **Risks (confirmed from text):**
    *   **Extensive Litigation:** Significant legal liabilities are the dominant risk. The 10-K's "Cautionary Note" lists liabilities related to PFAS and certain fluorochemicals as a material factor (2023-02-08). Additional context confirms 3,511 PFAS-related cases as of December 2022 (2023-03-01) and a "growing legal battle" over combat-grade earplugs, with analysts projecting large potential liabilities (2023-03-22). A financial commentary directly lists "too many litigation risks" as a reason for caution (2023-03-31).
    *   **Regulatory and Environmental Costs:** Operations are subject to extensive environmental laws and regulations. The company plans substantial capital expenditures for environmental compliance, estimated at approximately $646 million in aggregate for 2023 and 2024 (2023-02-08).
    *   **Execution & Competition:** The 10-K states the company's products "are subject to competition from products manufactured and sold by other technologically oriented companies" (2023-02-08). Forward-looking statements warn of risks from operational execution and inability to generate productivity improvements (2023-02-08).
*   **Potential Offsets (confirmed from text):**
    *   **Portfolio Strength & Diversification:** The business description outlines a diversified portfolio of strong brands across multiple industrial and consumer end markets, which the company identifies as a competitive advantage (2023-02-08).
    *   **Strategic Restructuring:** The announced spin-off of the Health Care segment (2023-02-08) is a corporate action that could be viewed as a potential catalyst to unlock value.
    *   **Market Position:** Some financial commentary suggests growth opportunities in specific units like the auto OEM business and advanced materials (2023-03-30), and notes institutional ownership associated with index inclusion (2023-03-22).

**4. Concise Qualitative Assessment: Negative**
The confirmed evidence points to a heavily burdened operational and legal backdrop. The primary qualitative factor is the material overhang from large-scale litigation (PFAS, earplugs), which dominates company risk disclosures and recent financial commentary. This is coupled with meaningful regulatory and environmental compliance costs. While the company possesses a diversified industrial franchise and is undertaking strategic actions like the Health Care spin-off, the scale and uncertainty of the legal liabilities present a substantial and ongoing challenge to the business narrative, overshadowing potential near-term operational improvements cited in some reports.

corrections.issue: Quant report states 'Gross margin contracted to 43.2% in Q4 ($3.49B/$8.08B) vs. 45.4% in Q1 2022.' Q1 2022 gross margin from raw data is $4.00B/$8.83B = 45.3%, which is close but the report rounds to 45.4%. More importantly, the report does not note that Q1 2022 was not the peak — Q1 2021 gross margin was $4.33B/$8.85B = 48.9%, and the trend of margin compression spans the full 8-quarter window, not just a Q4 vs Q1 2022 comparison. This is a minor framing issue, does not materially change the direction.
corrections.required_action: No action required; the directional conclusion (margin compression) is correct. The comparison is illustrative rather than misleading.

corrections.issue: Quant report states 'TTM net margin is 16.9%' and separately 'The sharp disconnect between TTM net income ($5.79B) and recent quarterly results ($543.00M) indicates poor durability of reported earnings.' The TTM net margin of 16.9% ($5.79B/$34.23B) is arithmetically correct but is heavily inflated by the Q3 2022 result ($3.86B net income on $8.62B revenue, a 44.8% net margin quarter). The report correctly flags the volatility but does not explicitly call out that the Q3 2022 quarter alone accounts for ~67% of TTM net income, which is the single most important earnings quality observation. The raw data shows Q3 2022 operating income of $4.16B on $8.62B revenue (48.3% operating margin) — an extreme outlier vs. all other quarters (~$0.1B–$2.0B operating income). This likely reflects a large one-time gain (possibly a legal settlement reversal or divestiture gain) that the quant report alludes to but never identifies.
corrections.required_action: PM should note that TTM earnings are dominated by a single anomalous quarter (Q3 2022) and the 'normalized' earnings run-rate is far below the TTM figure. The 16.9% TTM net margin is not representative of ongoing profitability.

corrections.issue: Qual report states 'analysts projecting large potential liabilities' for earplug litigation, citing a Mizuho note. The raw evidence from the CNBC article (2023-03-22) quotes Mizuho's Brett Linzey saying 'even the low end of previously settled Combat Arms lawsuits (or even half that amount) equates to some pretty healthy liabilities 3M may have to absorb.' The qual report's characterization is directionally consistent with the source but slightly overstates certainty — the source uses 'may have to absorb' language, not a definitive projection. This is a minor nuance.
corrections.required_action: No action required; the litigation risk characterization is directionally accurate.

unsupported_claims: Quant report claims 'Cash Flow: Data unavailable for all periods' — this is consistent with the raw data which shows '(no data)' for all cash flow quarters. This is accurate, not unsupported.

Quant report states 'operating margin collapsed to 7.8% in Q4 ($632.00M/$8.08B) vs. 18.5% in Q1 2022.' Q1 2022 operating margin from raw data: $1.64B/$8.83B = 18.6%, which rounds to 18.5% or 18.6%. The 7.8% Q4 figure is correct ($632M/$8.08B = 7.8%). The comparison is accurate.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: MMM trades at historically depressed valuations (P/E 8.6, P/B 3.4) with a deleveraging balance sheet, but extreme earnings volatility—where Q3 2022 alone accounts for ~67% of TTM net income—massive unquantified litigation liabilities (PFAS, earplugs), and declining revenue/margins create a deeply uncertain risk/reward that prevents a confident directional call.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: MMM trades at historically depressed valuations (P/E 8.6, P/B 3.4) with a deleveraging balance sheet, but extreme earnings volatility—where Q3 2022 alone accounts for ~67% of TTM net income—massive unquantified litigation liabilities (PFAS, earplugs), and declining revenue/margins create a deeply uncertain risk/reward that prevents a confident directional call.
key_factors: Valuation at historical lows: P/E of 8.633 (12-month range 8.1–17.5) and P/B of 3.377 (12-month range 3.2–5.3) as of 2023-03-31, with the stock at $87.88 near its 52-week low of $83.74, suggesting significant bad news is priced in.

Earnings dominated by a single anomalous quarter: Q3 2022 net income of $3.86B (44.8% net margin) accounts for ~67% of TTM net income ($5.79B), while Q4 2022 net income collapsed to $543M and operating margin fell to 7.8%—the TTM 16.9% net margin is not representative of ongoing profitability.

Massive unquantified litigation overhang: 3,511 PFAS-related cases as of December 2022 (10-K, 2023-02-08) and growing earplug litigation with analysts citing potential for 'pretty healthy liabilities' (Mizuho, 2023-03-22), creating a binary risk that is difficult to model.

Revenue in structural decline: Q4 2022 revenue of $8.08B was down -6.3% QoQ and -6.2% YoY, with TTM revenue of $34.23B, while gross margin compressed from 48.9% (Q1 2021) to 43.2% (Q4 2022) across the full 8-quarter window.

Item 1. Business
General
Cisco designs and sells a broad range of technologies that power the Internet. We are integrating our platforms across networking, security, collaboration, applications and the cloud. These platforms are designed to help our customers manage more users, devices and things connecting to their networks. This will enable us to provide customers with a highly secure, intelligent platform for their digital business.
We conduct our business globally and manage our business by geography. Our business is organized into the following three geographic segments: Americas; Europe, Middle East, and Africa (EMEA); and Asia Pacific, Japan, and China (APJC).
Our products and technologies are grouped into the following categories: Secure, Agile Networks; Internet for the Future; Collaboration; End-to-End Security; Optimized Application Experiences; and Other Products. In addition to our product offerings, we provide a broad range of service offerings, including technical support services and advanced services. Increasingly, we are delivering our technologies through software and services. Our customers include businesses of all sizes, public institutions, governments, and service providers, including large webscale providers. These customers often look to us as a strategic partner to help them use information technology (IT) to differentiate themselves and drive positive business outcomes.
We were incorporated in California in 1984 and reincorporated in Delaware in 2021. Our headquarters are in San Jose, California. The mailing address of our headquarters is 170 West Tasman Drive, San Jose, California 95134-1706, and our telephone number at that location is (408) 526-4000. Our website is www.cisco.com. Through a link on the Investor Relations section of our website, we make available the following filings as soon as reasonably practicable after they are electronically filed with or furnished to the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) at sec.gov: our Annual Report on Form 10-K, Quarterly Reports on Form 10-Q, Current Reports on Form 8-K, and any amendments to those reports or other information filed or furnished pursuant to Section 13(a) or 15(d) of the Exchange Act. All such filings are available free of charge. The information published on our website, or any other website referenced herein, is not incorporated into this report.
Strategy and Priorities
As our customers add billions of new connections to their enterprises, and as more applications move to a multicloud environment, the network becomes even more critical. Our customers are navigating change at an unprecedented pace. In this dynamic environment, we believe their priorities are to reimagine applications, power hybrid work, transform infrastructure, and secure the enterprise.
Our strategy is to help our customers connect, secur
…[truncated 19164 chars]…
hardware support to include software, solutions, and premium support.
We also provide comprehensive advisory services that are focused on responsive, preventive, and consultative support of our technologies for specific networking needs. We are investing in and expanding advisory services in the areas of software, cloud, security, and analytics, which reflects our strategy of selling customer outcomes. We are focused on three priorities: utilizing technology advisory services to drive higher product and services; assessment and migration services providing the tools, expertise and methodologies to enable our customers to migrate to new technology platforms; and providing optimization services aligned with customers' business expectations.
5
Table of Contents
Customers and Markets
Many factors influence the IT, collaboration, and networking requirements of our customers. These include the size of the organization, number and types of technology systems, geographic location, and business applications deployed throughout the customer's network. Our customer base is not limited to any specific industry, geography, or market segment. Our customers primarily operate in the following markets: enterprise, commercial, service provider, and public sector.
Enterprise
Enterprise businesses are large regional, national, or global organizations with multiple locations or branch offices. Many enterprise businesses have unique IT, collaboration, and networking needs within a multivendor environment. We offer service and support packages, financing, and managed network services, primarily through our service provider partners. We sell these products through a network of third-party application and technology vendors and channel partners, as well as selling directly to these customers.
Commercial
The commercial market represents larger, or midmarket and small businesses. We sell to our midmarket customers through a combination of our direct sales force and channel partners. These customers typically require the latest advanced technologies that our enterprise customers demand, but with less complexity. Small businesses require information technologies and communications products that are easy to configure, install, and maintain. We sell to these smaller organizations within the commercial market primarily through channel partners.
Service Providers
Service providers offer data, voice, video, and mobile/wireless services to businesses, governments, utilities, and consumers worldwide. The service provider market includes regional, national, and international wireline carriers, webscale operators as well as Internet, cable, and wireless providers. We also include media, broadcast, and content providers within our service provider market, as the lines in the telecommunications industry continue to blur between traditional network-based, content-based and application-based services. Service providers use a variety of our products and services for their own networks. In addition, many service providers use Cisco data center, virtualization, and collaboration technologies to offer managed or Internet-based services to their business customers. Compared with other customers, service providers are more likely to require network design, deployment, and support services because of the greater scale and higher complexity of their networks, whose requirements are addressed, we believe, by our architectural approach.
Public Sector
The public sector market includes federal, state and local governments, as well as educational institution customers. Many public sector customers have unique IT, collaboration, and networking needs within a multi-vendor environment. We sell to public sector customers through a network of third-party application and technology vendors, channel partners, as well as through direct sales.
Sales Overview
As of the end of fiscal 2022, our worldwide sales and marketing functions consisted of approximately 26,000 employees, including managers, sales representatives, and technical support personnel. We sell our products and services both directly and through a variety of channels with support from our salesforce. A substantial portion of our products and services is sold through channel partners, and the remainder is sold through direct sales. Channel partners include systems integrators, service providers, other resellers, and distributors.
Systems integrators and service providers typically sell directly to end users and often provide system installation, technical support, professional services, and other support services in addition to network equipment sales. Systems integrators also typically integrate our products into an overall solution. Some service providers are also systems integrators.
Distributors may hold inventory and sell to systems integrators, service providers, and other resellers. We refer to sales through distributors as our two-tier system of sales to the end customer. Revenue from two-tier distributors is recognized based on a sell-in method. These distributors may be given business terms that allow them to return a limited portion of inventory, receive credits for changes in selling prices, receive certain rebates, and participate in various cooperative marketing programs.
For information regarding risks related to our sales channels, see "Item 1A. Risk Factors," including the risk factors entitled "Disruption of or changes in our distribution model could harm our sales and margins" and "Inventory management relating to our sales to our two-tier distribution channel is complex, and excess inventory may harm our gross margins."
For information regarding risks relating to our international operations, see "Item 1A. Risk Factors," including the risk factors entitled "Our operating results may be adversely affected by unfavorable economic and market conditions and the uncertain

geopolitical environment;" "Entrance into new or developing markets exposes us to additional competition and will likely increase demands on our service and support operations;" "Due to the global nature of our operations, political or economic changes or other factors in a specific country or region could harm our operating results and financial condition;" "We are exposed to fluctuations in currency exchange rates that could negatively impact our financial results and cash flows;" and "Cyber attacks, data breaches or malware may disrupt our operations, harm our operating results and financial condition, and damage our reputation or otherwise materially harm our business; and cyber attacks or data breaches on our customers' or third-party providers' networks, or in cloud-based services provided to, by, or enabled by us, could result in claims of liability against us, give rise to legal and/or regulatory action, damage our reputation or otherwise materially harm our business," among others.
Our service offerings complement our products through a range of consulting, technical, project, quality, and software maintenance services, including 24-hour online and telephone support through technical assistance centers.

We provide financing arrangements for certain qualified customers to build, maintain, and upgrade their networks. We believe customer financing is a competitive advantage in obtaining business, particularly for those customers involved in significant infrastructure projects. CSCO.quantitative_report:
### 1. Financial Trajectory (Quarterly & TTM)
*   **Revenue:** Stable with modest growth. Q2 2023 (Jan 28) revenue was **13.59B**, a **+6.9%** YoY increase from **12.72B**. The TTM revenue sum is **53.16B**.
*   **Earnings:** Slight softening. Q2 2023 EPS diluted was **0.67**, down **-5.6%** YoY from **0.71**. TTM net income is **11.30B**.
*   **Margins:** Stable gross margins, slight compression in operating margins. Q2 2023 gross margin was **62.0%** (8.43B/13.59B), operating margin was **24.2%** (3.29B/13.59B). TTM operating income is **13.88B**.
*   **Cash Flow:** No quarterly cash flow data is supplied.
*   **Balance Sheet:** Consistently strong. Total equity grew QoQ from **40.27B** (Oct 29, 2022) to **41.47B** (Jan 28, 2023). Total debt decreased from **9.50B** (Jan 29, 2022) to **8.90B** (Jan 28, 2023).

### 2. Earnings Quality & Balance-Sheet Resilience
*   **Earnings Quality:** Supported by strong, stable margins. Net margin for the quarter is **20.4%** (2.77B/13.59B). The net margin as of March 31, 2023 valuation data is **0.213** (21.3%). The YoY EPS decline of **-5.6%** against +6.9% revenue growth suggests rising costs, as cost of revenue increased from **4.67B** to **5.17B** YoY.
*   **Balance-Sheet Resilience:** High. Debt-to-equity ratio (total debt/total equity) is **0.215** (8.90B / 41.47B). The company has reduced debt from **11.50B** (Jul 31, 2021) to **8.90B** while growing equity.

### 3. Valuation vs. Company History
*   **P/E Diluted:** At **19.04** on 2023-03-31, it is at the upper end of its 1-year window (**13.9 to 19.9**). It has expanded from a trough of **14.65** in October 2022.
*   **P/B:** At **5.19** on 2023-03-31, it is above the midpoint of its 1-year window (**4.1 to 5.9**).
*   **Trend:** Both the P/E and P/B have been rising since late 2022. Net margin and ROE have compressed slightly over the same period (net margin from **0.229** to **0.213**, ROE from **0.299** to **0.273**).

### 4. Price Level & Return Context vs. Fundamentals
*   **Price:** Close of **52.27** on 2023-03-31. The 1-year range is **38.60 to 56.88**.
*   **Returns:** Quarterly return (from Dec 29, 2022) is **+10.1%**. Annual return (from Mar 30, 2022) is **-6.7%**.
*   **Fundamental Link:** The annual return of **-6.7%** contrasts with TTM revenue growth of approximately **+6.9%** (YoY Q2) and a stable but slightly pressured earnings profile. The recent quarterly price appreciation coincides with a rebound from a valuation trough (P/E from ~14.6 to ~19.0) and recovery from a price low of **40.57** in September 2022.

### 5. Strongest Quantitative Upside & Downside
*   **Upside:** **Balance sheet strength** and **consistent cash generation implied by stable profitability** (TTM net income 11.30B). The low debt-to-equity of **0.215** provides financial flexibility. If margin pressure stabilizes, the current elevated valuation could be sustained.
*   **Downside:** **Valuation expansion without proportional earnings growth.** The P/E of **19.04** is near the top of its range while YoY EPS growth is negative at **-5.6%**. The lack of supplied cash flow data prevents a direct assessment of cash conversion, which is a data gap.

### 6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion
The stock presents a mixed quantitative profile. **Positively**, it has a pristine balance sheet (debt-to-equity **0.215**), stable revenue, and profitability. **Negatively**, its valuation (P/E **19.04**) has expanded to the top of its recent range while earnings per share have declined year-over-year (**-5.6%**). The recent price recovery (**+10.1%** quarterly) appears more driven by multiple expansion from a trough than by fundamental earnings acceleration. The risk/reward appears balanced but leans cautious, as the current valuation prices in continued stability but leaves limited margin of safety for any further deterioration in earnings quality or growth trajectory.
CSCO.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Report: Cisco Systems, Inc. (CSCO)**
**Decision Date: 2023-04-01**

**1. Business/Franchise Read**
Cisco designs and sells a broad range of technologies that power the Internet, integrating platforms across networking, security, collaboration, applications, and the cloud. The company operates globally through three geographic segments (Americas, EMEA, APJC). Its business is organized into product categories including Secure, Agile Networks (switching, routing, wireless, compute), Internet for the Future (routed optical networking, silicon, optics), Collaboration (Webex suite, devices, contact center), End-to-End Security, and Optimized Application Experiences (full-stack observability). A key strategic focus is the ongoing transformation of its business model towards software and subscription-based offerings (e.g., Cisco DNA, Cisco Plus) to provide flexible consumption models. The company serves a diverse customer base across enterprise, commercial, service provider, and public sector markets.

**2. Recent Developments (Dated)**
*   **2023-02-15:** Cisco reported Q2 FY2023 earnings, stating it "continue[d] to execute well, delivering better than expected results in revenue, record non-GAAP EPS and operating cash flow." The company raised its full-year outlook.
*   **2023-02-16:** Analysis noted Cisco's Q2 revenue of $13.6 billion grew 7.1% year-over-year, outpacing the S&P 500 average and consensus estimates.
*   **2023-02-21:** Based on accelerating revenue growth and stabilizing margins, Cisco issued guidance for adjusted EPS to rise 10%-13% year-over-year in Q3 and 11%-12% for the full fiscal year.
*   **2023-03-22:** Multiple articles identified Cisco as a tech giant positioned to help companies use AI to cut costs and automate, citing its innovative strategies and strong financials.
*   **2023-03-23:** A California federal judge dismissed Cisco's trade secrets lawsuit against a former employee and Plantronics (Poly), ruling Cisco failed to show a threat of misappropriation.

**3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets**
*   **Risk: Legal and Intellectual Property Challenges.** The loss of the trade secrets case (2023-03-23) highlights potential difficulties in protecting IP and managing competitive threats from former employees. The 10-K also lists general risks related to cybersecurity attacks and data breaches.
*   **Risk: Execution in a Shifting Market.** The 10-K explicitly states a risk that failure to predict and respond to emerging technological trends (e.g., AI, cloud-native applications) could harm operating results and market share. Competition is noted as a factor in new markets.
*   **Risk: Macroeconomic and Customer Spending Uncertainty.** News from 2023-03-31 on the Silicon Valley Bank implosion and "zombie VCs" suggests a tightening funding environment for tech startups, a potential customer segment. The 10-K cites risks from unfavorable economic conditions and geopolitical environment.
*   **Offset: Strong Execution and Positive Outlook.** Recent earnings (2023-02-15) demonstrated better-than-expected performance and a raised full-year outlook, indicating solid near-term execution. The strategic shift to software and subscriptions is designed to create more predictable revenue streams.
*   **Offset: Positioned in Key Growth Areas.** The company is actively investing in and cited as a beneficiary of secular trends like hybrid work, infrastructure transformation, and enterprise AI adoption (2023-03-22), which align with its core product and strategy pillars.

**4. Qualitative Assessment**
**Positive.** The supplied evidence points to a company executing well on its strategic transformation, as evidenced by its recent earnings beat and raised guidance. Its franchise is deeply embedded in critical enterprise infrastructure, and it is actively aligning its portfolio with key secular growth themes (hybrid work, cloud, security, AI). While legal setbacks and macroeconomic uncertainties present tangible risks, the company's scale, installed base, and strategic focus on software provide meaningful offsets. The narrative as of early 2023 is one of operational strength and strategic relevance.

corrections.issue: The quant report states 'Q2 2023 revenue was 13.59B, a +6.9% YoY increase from 12.72B' and separately the qual report states 'Q2 revenue of $13.6 billion grew 7.1% year-over-year.' The raw quant data shows revenue=13.59B for Q2 2023 and revenue=12.72B for Q2 2022, yielding (13.59-12.72)/12.72 = 6.84%, which rounds to +6.8% or +6.9%. The 7.1% figure in the qual report comes from the web context news article (2023-02-16) and is not supported by the raw financial data. The qual report should not present the 7.1% figure as a standalone fact without attribution to the news source.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: Cisco presents a balanced risk-reward with a pristine balance sheet and solid execution offset by valuation that has expanded to the top of its range while earnings growth remains negative. The stock is fairly valued at current levels, offering limited upside without a meaningful earnings acceleration.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: Cisco presents a balanced risk-reward with a pristine balance sheet and solid execution offset by valuation that has expanded to the top of its range while earnings growth remains negative. The stock is fairly valued at current levels, offering limited upside without a meaningful earnings acceleration.
key_factors: Valuation stretched near top of range: P/E of 19.04 on 2023-03-31 sits at the upper end of its 1-year window (13.9-19.9), having expanded from a trough of 14.65 in October 2022, leaving limited margin of safety.

Earnings declining despite revenue growth: Q2 2023 EPS diluted fell -5.6% YoY to 0.67 while revenue grew +6.9% to 13.59B, indicating cost pressures as cost of revenue rose from 4.67B to 5.17B and operating income declined from 3.49B to 3.29B.

Balance sheet strength provides downside protection: Debt-to-equity ratio of 0.215 (8.90B debt vs 41.47B equity) with total debt reduced from 11.50B in July 2021, and TTM net income of 11.30B supports stable profitability.

Positive strategic execution and raised guidance: Q2 FY2023 earnings beat expectations with 2023-02-15 results prompting raised full-year outlook, and the company is positioned in secular growth themes including AI, hybrid work, and security.

Latest 10-K business section (newest first):
  filing_date=2022-11-16 section=business period_end=2022-09-30
ITEM 1.    Business
OVERVIEW
Visa is one of the world's leaders in digital payments. Our purpose is to uplift everyone, everywhere by being the best way to pay and be paid. We facilitate global commerce and money movement across more than 200 countries and territories among a global set of consumers, merchants, financial institutions and government entities through innovative technologies.
Since Visa's early days in 1958, we have been in the business of facilitating payments between consumers and businesses. As a trusted engine of commerce and with new ways to pay, we are working to provide payment solutions for everyone, everywhere. We are focused on extending, enhancing and investing in our proprietary network, VisaNet, to offer a single connection point for facilitating payment transactions to multiple endpoints through various form factors. Through our network, we offer products, solutions and services that facilitate secure, reliable and efficient money movement for participants in the ecosystem.
- We facilitate secure, reliable and efficient money movement among consumers, issuing and acquiring financial institutions, and merchants. We have traditionally referred to this as the "four-party" model. Please see Our Core Business discussion below. As the payments ecosystem continues to evolve, we have broadened this model to include digital banks, digital wallets and a range of financial technology companies (fintechs), governments and non-governmental organizations (NGOs). We provide transaction processing services (primarily authorization, clearing and settlement) to our financial institution and merchant clients through VisaNet, our advanced transaction processing network. During fiscal year 2022, we saw 258 billio
…[truncated 20268 chars]…
 added value to our clients and their customers, financial institutions, merchants and partners through compelling brand expressions, a wide range of products and services as well as innovative brand and marketing efforts. In line with our commitment to an expansive and diverse range of partnerships for the benefit of our stakeholders, Visa is the only brand in the world that is a top sponsor of FIFA, the
11
Table of Contents
Olympic Games, and the NFL and is one of the most active sponsors of women's football around the world.
Talent
Attracting, developing and advancing the best talent globally is critical to our continued success. This year we grew our total workforce from approximately 21,500 in fiscal year 2021 to approximately 26,500 employees in fiscal year 2022, an increase of 23 percent year over year. Voluntary workforce turnover (rolling 12-month attrition) was 12.1 percent as of September 30, 2022. Visa employees are located in more than 80 countries and territories, with 54 percent located outside the U.S. At the end of fiscal year 2022, Visa's global workforce was 58 percent men and 42 percent women, and women represented 36 percent of Visa's leadership (defined as vice president level and above). In the U.S., ethnicity of our workforce was 41 percent Asian, 8 percent Black, 12 percent Hispanic, 3 percent Other and 36 percent White. For our U.S. leadership, the breakdown was 18 percent Asian, 7 percent Black, 13 percent Hispanic, 2 percent Other and 60 percent White.
Given Visa's ambitious growth agenda, it is important to enable our employees to achieve their individual performance goals while also supporting personal career interests. This year we introduced several changes to career growth and planning at Visa, including new growth paths and tools that take into consideration the unique professional backgrounds, skills, accomplishments, and future performance goals of our employees. These tools support meaningful dialogue about performance and help drive development, retention and growth of top talent in a highly competitive talent market.
We have an unwavering commitment to valuing the unique identities of our employees and their contributions to Visa. In 2020, we established the Stand Together initiative in support of social justice and racial equality in the U.S. focused on our people, our community and our company. We are proud of the progress we have made. Our partnership with the Thurgood Marshall College Fund for the Visa Black Scholars and Jobs Program resulted in Visa's inaugural class of 51 scholars participating in year-round programs and training aimed at developing their professional and technical skills this past year. We also welcomed the second cohort of 75 scholars this fall. Upon graduation, all scholars who have met their commitments will be offered a full-time job with Visa.
We continue to increase the number of underrepresented employees in the U.S. We are committed to recruiting and retaining diverse talent through employee development programs aimed at advancing their careers at Visa. As a company, we continue to partner with historically Black colleges and a generally more diverse set of universities to further develop our talent pipeline. Visa is committed to pay equity, regardless of gender or race/ethnicity, and conducts pay equity analyses on an annual basis. More details regarding our human capital management, as well as enhanced workforce disclosures that include our 2021 Consolidated EEO-1 Report and our 2021 Environmental, Social and Governance (ESG) Report, can be found on our website at visa.com/esg. See Available Information below.
For additional information, please see the section titled "Talent and Human Capital Management" in Visa's 2022 Proxy Statement.
FINTECH AND DIGITAL PARTNERSHIPS
Fintechs are key enablers of new payment experiences and new flows. Our work with fintechs has opened new points of acceptance, extended credit at the point of sale, made cross-border money flows more efficient, moved B2B spend onto Visa's network, expedited payroll and provided digital wallet customers access to our services.
To better serve fintechs, Visa has a suite of streamlined commercial programs and digital onboarding tools. Our Fintech Fast Track program enables qualifying fintechs to quickly launch and scale their programs. The program has welcomed hundreds of fintechs who are actively engaged in the program.
With our startup engagement programs, the Visa Everywhere Initiative and the Inclusive Fintech 50, early-stage companies can build payment solutions based on our capabilities. With the global Visa Fintech Partner Connect program, we are helping our clients tap into innovations emerging from the fintech community. The program is designed to help financial institutions quickly connect with a curated set of technology providers, helping Visa's issuing partners create digital-first experiences.
MERGERS AND ACQUISITIONS, JOINT VENTURES AND STRATEGIC INVESTMENTS
Visa continually explores opportunities to augment our capabilities and provide meaningful value to our clients. Mergers and acquisitions, joint ventures and strategic investments complement our internal development and
12
Table of Contents
enhance our partnerships to align with Visa's priorities. Visa applies a rigorous business analysis to our acquisitions, joint ventures and investments to ensure they will differentiate our network, provide value added services and accelerate growth.
In fiscal year 2022, we acquired The Currency Cloud Group Limited (Currencycloud), a global platform that enables financial institutions and fintechs to provide innovative, cross-border foreign exchange solutions. We also acquired Tink, an open banking platform that enables financial institutions, fintechs and merchants to build financial products and services and move money.
ENVIRONMENTAL, SOCIAL AND GOVERNANCE
As a trusted brand, Visa has an opportunity and responsibility to contribute to a more inclusive, equitable and sustainable world. We believe that economies that include everyone everywhere, uplift everyone everywhere. As we build business resilience and long-term value, we are committed to managing the risks and opportunities that arise from ESG issues. We are focused on empowering people and economies; securing commerce and protecting customers; investing in our workforce; protecting the planet; and operating responsibly. Our 2021 ESG report, as well as other ESG-related resources are available on our website at visa.com/esg. See Available Information below.
INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY
We own and manage the Visa brand, which stands for acceptance, security, convenience, speed and reliability. Our portfolio of Visa-owned trademarks is important to our business. Generally, trademark registrations are valid indefinitely as long as they are in use and/or maintained. We give our clients access to these assets through agreements with our issuers and acquirers, which authorize the use of our trademarks in connection with their participation in our payments network. Additionally, we own a number of patents and patent applications related to our business and continue to pursue patents in emerging technologies that may have applications in our business. We rely on a combination of patent, trademark, copyright and trade secret laws in the U.S. and other jurisdictions, as well as confidentiality procedures and contractual provisions, to protect our proprietary technology.
COMPETITION
The global payments industry continues to undergo dynamic change. Existing and emerging competitors compete with Visa's network and payment solutions for consumers and for participation by financial institutions and merchants. Technology and innovation are shifting consumer habits and driving growth opportunities in ecommerce, mobile payments, blockchain technology and digital currencies. These advances are enabling new entrants, many of which depa

**Quantitative Report: V (Decision Date 2023-04-01)**

1. **Revenue, Earnings, Margin, Cash-Flow, Balance-Sheet Trajectory**  
   - Revenue: Quarterly revenue increased from 5.73B (2021-03-31) to 7.94B (2022-12-31), with YoY growth of +12.4% in the latest quarter. TTM revenue is 30.19B.  
   - Earnings: Net income in the latest quarter is 4.18B, with YoY growth of +5.6%. TTM net income is 15.18B.  
   - Margins: Net margin is stable at 0.503-0.516 over the past year; operating margin in the latest quarter is 0.634.  
   - Cash-Flow: Cash flow data is unavailable for all quarters.  
   - Balance-Sheet: Total assets increased to 85.39B (2022-12-31) from 80.19B (2021-03-31). Total equity rose to 36.92B from 35.58B in the previous quarter. Total debt in 2021 ranged from 20.92B to 21.00B, but recent quarters lack debt data.

2. **Earnings Quality and Balance-Sheet Resilience**  
   - Earnings Quality: High net margin (0.503) and ROE (0.411) indicate strong profitability. However, cash flow data is unavailable, preventing assessment of cash conversion.  
   - Balance-Sheet Resilience: Equity ratio is 36.92B / 85.39B ≈ 43.2%. Liabilities decreased from 49.92B to 48.46B in the latest quarter, with equity increasing, suggesting improved resilience. Debt levels are not disclosed for recent periods.

3. **Valuation versus History**  
   - Valuation ratios (pe_diluted, ev_to_ebit, fcf_yield, p_b) are unavailable. Net margin slightly decreased from 0.516 (2022-04-01) to 0.503 (2023-03-31), while ROE increased from 0.363 to 0.411 over the same period.

4. **Price Level and Quarterly/Annual Return Context versus Fundamental Path**  
   - Price: Latest close is 225.46 (2023-03-31), with a quarterly return of +8.4% (from 208.06 on 2022-12-29) and an annual return of +0.7% (from 223.95 on 2022-03-30). The 52-week range is 174.60 to 250.58.  
   - Fundamental Path: Revenue YoY growth (+12.4%) and net income YoY growth (+5.6%) contrast with the modest annual price return (+0.7%), indicating potential underperformance relative to fundamentals, though recent quarterly momentum is positive.

5. **Strongest Quantitative Upside and Downside**  
   - Upside: Strong revenue growth (+12.4% YoY), high and improving ROE (0.411), and recent price recovery (+8.4% quarterly).  
   - Downside: Net income growth (+5.6%) lags revenue growth, suggesting margin compression. Price is 11% below its 52-week high (250.58), and cash flow data is missing.

V.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Report: Visa Inc. (V)**
**Decision Date: 2023-04-01**

**1. Business/Franchise Read from Supplied Narrative**
Visa operates as a global leader in digital payments, facilitating transactions across more than 200 countries and territories. Its core business is built on a "four-party" model connecting consumers, merchants, financial institutions, and acquirers through its proprietary VisaNet network. The company is evolving into a "network of networks" to enable money movement across all available networks and form factors. Its strategy focuses on three growth pillars: accelerating consumer payments (credit, debit, prepaid), capturing new flows (e.g., Visa Direct for P2P/B2C, Visa B2B Connect for cross-border B2B), and expanding value-added services (e.g., issuing/acceptance solutions, risk/identity, open banking via Tink). Visa's scale is significant, processing 193 billion transactions in FY2022, with 4.1 billion credentials in circulation. The franchise is fortified by its global brand, technology platforms, and security infrastructure.

**2. Recent Developments (Dated)**
*   **Legal Settlement (2023-03-15):** A U.S. appeals court upheld a $5.6 billion antitrust settlement with retailers over interchange fees, concluding a long-standing multidistrict litigation.
*   **New Legal Action (2023-03-14):** Mastercard and Visa face fresh competition lawsuits in the UK, with a law firm filing a large claim related to multilateral interchange fees.
*   **Market Sentiment & Estimates (2023-03-14):** Visa was highlighted as a trending stock, with Zacks Consensus Estimates projecting year-over-year revenue growth of 7.72% for the upcoming quarter.
*   **Stock Performance (2023-03-28):** Visa's stock dipped more than the broader market in a single trading session.
*   **Growth Catalysts Noted (2023-02-11, 2023-03-06):** Analyst context analysis identified the long-term shift from cash, the potential in emerging markets as a key growth driver, noted the company's recovery from the 2020 pandemic impact.

 subsequent travel rebound.

**3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets**
*   **Risks:**
    *   **Regulatory & Legal:** The upheld $5.6B settlement represent a material financial resolution to past conduct. ongoing UK lawsuits indicate persistent legal exposure to interchange fee structures. The 10-K explicitly notes competition from new entrants (fintechs, digital currencies, blockchain) and regulatory changes.
    *   **Competition & Disruption:** The 10-K details dynamic industry change, with technology enabling new entrants that may bypass traditional card networks.
*   **Macroeconomic Sensitivity:** While not experiencing pronounced seasonality, Visa's business is tied to global consumer and business spending volumes, which are sensitive to economic cycles.

*   **Offsets:**
    *   **Network Scale & Diversification:** Its vast global network, processing volume, and "network of networks" strategy create high barriers to entry and multiple growth vectors beyond core consumer payments.
    *   **Strategic Investments:** Acquisitions like Tink (open banking) and Currencycloud (FX) position it to capture new flows and embed itself in evolving financial infrastructure.
    *   **Brand & Trust:** Its strong, globally recognized brand is a core asset that supports client and consumer adoption.

**4. Concise Qualitative Assessment**
**Positive.** Visa's franchise is dominant, with a clear strategic roadmap to extend its leadership into new payment flows and value-added services. Recent legal settlements, while costly, remove a significant overhang. The primary risks are regulatory and competitive, but the company's scale, network effects, and strategic investments provide substantial offsets. The narrative points to a resilient business with multiple levers for long-term growth.

corrections.required_action: Note that ROE peaked at 0.42 in late 2022 and has since declined to 0.411; this recent softening should be flagged alongside the longer-term improvement narrative.

corrections.issue: Qual report states 'A U.S. appeals court upheld a $5.6 billion antitrust settlement with retailers over interchange fees, concluding a long-standing multiditrust litigation.' The raw evidence from topclassactions.com (2023-03-23) says 'Partial class action status has been granted' and the robinskaplan.com source (2023-03-15) describes it as 'the largest settlement of a private antitrust action.' The qual report's characterization of the litigation as 'concluded' may overstate finality — the raw evidence references partial class action status and an ongoing MDL, and the fresh UK lawsuits (2023-03-14) indicate continued legal exposure.
corrections.required_action: Clarify that the $5.6B settlement was upheld but the underlying MDL may not be fully concluded, and that fresh UK competition claims represent additional unresolved legal risk.

corrections.issue: Quant report notes 'Cash flow data is unavailable for all quarters' and flags this as a risk, but the conclusion only says 'pending additional valuation and cash generation evidence.' The raw packs confirm zero cash flow data points across all 8 quarters. This is a material gap for a company trading at premium multiples — the neutral stance appropriately tempers conviction, but the PM should be explicitly warned that the absence of FCF data prevents any independent cash-based valuation.
corrections.required_action: Explicitly flag that the complete absence of cash flow data across all quarters prevents FCF-based valuation and cash conversion assessment, which is a significant analytical limitation for a premium-valued name.
